question: An OWI with the EAN Specialty Instructor rating may certify divers in:

options.b: EAN up to 40% (recreational nitrox)

explanation: Recreational nitrox tops out at 40% O2; technical training covers higher mixes.

question: SSI's XR (Extended Range) programs cover:

options.b: Technical disciplines including deeper, decompression, trimix and rebreather

explanation: XR is SSI's technical-diving line covering tec-rec through full trimix and CCR.

question: Coaching a struggling AI candidate during their training is best done by:

options.b: Private, specific feedback with practice opportunities and clear goals

explanation: Private, specific, practice-oriented feedback respects the candidate and accelerates improvement.

question: Adult learners are typically motivated by:

options.b: Internal motivators: self-improvement, problem-solving, achieving goals

explanation: Adult motivation is largely intrinsic; help them see how training meets their goals.

question: At the start of any course, the instructor should:

options.b: Introduce themselves, the course, expectations, schedule and safety

explanation: Course introduction sets expectations and reduces student anxiety.

question: At the end of a course, the instructor should:

options.b: Conduct a debrief, certify, log and recommend continuing-education paths

explanation: End-of-course wrap-up consolidates learning, certifies, and points the student toward next steps.

question: Students should leave a course with:

options.a: Verified, signed log entries for their training dives
options.b: No record

explanation: Logged dives form the foundation of dive history. Always sign and verify.

question: Encouraging students to own and dive in their own equipment:

options.a: Is an SSI core principle (Total Diving System)
options.b: Is irrelevant

explanation: Own-gear philosophy supports comfort, safety and student retention — central to SSI.

question: When recommending equipment, instructors should:

options.a: Sell the most expensive gear regardless of need
options.b: Recommend equipment that fits the student's diving and budget honestly

explanation: Honest, needs-based recommendations build trust and long-term loyalty.

question: Components of the SSI Instructor Training Course (ITC) include:

options.a: Standards & procedures, teaching academic, teaching in water, evaluation criteria, business of diving
options.b: Only physics

explanation: ITC is a comprehensive program preparing candidates for the IE and professional teaching.

question: An evaluator scoring an in-water teaching presentation looks for:

options.a: Briefing quality, demonstration quality, supervision/positioning, communication and debrief
options.b: Speed only

explanation: All teaching elements are scored; weak demos or supervision drop overall score significantly.

question: OWD students must learn standard hand signals including:

options.a: OK, problem, up, down, low on air, out of air, share air, buddy-up, ear-clear
options.b: Only OK

explanation: A complete set of basic signals must be mastered for safe diving.

question: For some advanced confined-water exercises (e.g., CESA simulation), depth should be:

options.a: Sufficient to allow the simulation realistically (e.g., 4-5 m where available)
options.b: 0.5 m only

explanation: Adequate depth makes simulations meaningful; very shallow water can't simulate ascent skills well.

question: The four OW training dives should be sequenced to:

options.b: Progressively introduce skills, build complexity, and incorporate fun/exploration time

explanation: Progressive sequencing matches the OWD performance requirements while including time to enjoy diving.

question: Best practice in briefing is to:

options.b: Be clear, concise, structured, with visual aids and check on understanding

explanation: Clear, structured briefings respect students' time and ensure understanding.

question: After every training dive, the instructor must:

options.a: Verify all divers safely return, log the dive and debrief
options.b: Leave immediately

explanation: Closure: head count, log dive details, debrief the experience.

question: When asked by a student about a standard, the instructor should:

options.b: Reference the official SSI Standards / Training Standards documents accurately

explanation: Knowing where to find current standards is part of professional duty.

question: If an instructor finds an error in SSI material, they should:

options.b: Continue with the correct information and report the issue to SSI for correction

explanation: Teach correctly, report errors via the proper channels for future updates.

question: If a student cannot master a skill after multiple attempts in one session, the instructor should:

options.b: Take a break, address fatigue/cold, and consider returning to the skill in a fresh session

explanation: Fatigue and stress impair learning. Reset and return rather than push through.

question: Certification of an OWD student should occur only when:

options.a: All performance requirements (academic, confined, OW) have been met to standards
options.b: The student has paid

explanation: Performance-based: only certify when ALL requirements are met.
